notes: Recording may be brittle — see assessment.json caveats. A real claude CLI rarely emits a >2 MB single JSONL line organically; we provoke a long line (5000+ word single-sentence reply). irrlicht's tailer (core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go) uses bufio.NewReaderSize + readLineCapped with a 64 MB cap (issue #270 regression hardening), so lines below 64 MB parse normally and lines above are logged-and-skipped past without stalling. Spec asserts only the state arc: the absence of a parser stall is INFERRED from the arc completing — if the tailer wedged on the long line, working→ready would never fire and the phase contract would time out.

text: Oversized assistant line is processed (or skipped past, under the 64 MB cap); terminal stop_reason lands; working → ready. The completion of this transition is the spec's parser-resilience proof.
